screenshot_note: Puppeteer failed again — chromium returned 'Resource temporarily unavailable: pthread_create' (thread exhaustion on the host). Screenshots reused from earlier today (10:19 AM, ~5h pre-review). Desktop 1280x800: All 5 workers at desks, vibe is 'Crushing It', ⚡ FULL SYNC banner active. Bloberto at CVO desk with crown and trophy. Top-right window element is a stark bright white rectangle (daytime sky bae6fd→f0f9ff against the dark office floor — bug confirmed again). Coffee corner shows 'COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT' overflowing the right edge of the office container. Conference table shows 'everyone is at their desk'. Idle characters appear visually contained within the office floor boundary. Mobile 375px: Same vibe. 'COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT' visibly clips beyond the right edge of the office floor. WindowElement bright rectangle present. Both tab labels render in full.
fixes_since_44: ONE confirmed fix: idle character wander keyframes now explicitly constrained with a clear code comment ('all within y: 73–87%, x: 14–72%') and the actual keyframe values confirm minimum left: 15%, maximum left: 71%, y: 73–87%. Characters no longer wander outside the office floor boundary. Screenshot confirms characters stay within the container. One fix. Out of twenty-two open items. Filing this under 'technically positive momentum'.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Stuck worker card renders 'undefined' emoji
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 511: `${stuckWorkers[0].emoji} ${stuckWorkers[0].name}` — emoji is undefined for every worker except Luna because normalize() spreads the raw object but workers.json only has emoji for Luna in DEFAULT_ROSTER. Screen shows literal 'undefined Carlos Working for Xh'. Fix: stuckWorkers[0].emoji ?? getRoleEmoji(stuckWorkers[0].role) using the existing ROLE_EMOJIS map in constants.js.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Progress bar fill width is NaN% for workers without progress field
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 235: `style={{ width: \`${Math.min(progress, 100)}%\` }}` — Math.min(undefined, 100) = NaN, producing invalid CSS 'NaN%'. The label (line 222) correctly guards with `typeof progress === 'number'`, but the fill div and the aria-label (line 231: `aria-label={\`Progress: ${progress}%\`}`) both lack the same guard. Fix: add `progress: typeof w.progress === 'number' ? w.progress : 0` to the normalize object in syncFromGitHub (App.jsx ~line 772).

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #43] Progress aria-label exposes 'undefined%' to screen readers
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 231: `aria-label={\`Progress: ${progress}%\`}` — when progress is undefined the label reads 'Progress: undefined%'. The aria-valuenow (line 228) correctly falls back to 0, but aria-label does not. Fix: `aria-label={\`Progress: ${typeof progress === 'number' ? progress : 0}%\`}`.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Date.now() in stuckWorkers useMemo captured once and goes stale
suggestions.description: StatsBar App.jsx lines 438-441: the filter uses Date.now() captured at memo creation time with dependency array [workers]. If a worker crosses the 2-hour stuck threshold between polls, the stuck badge won't appear until workers array reference changes. Fix: consume the existing useTimeTick(60_000) hook (already in scope at line 85) to force re-evaluation, or add a tick state as a useMemo dependency.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #44] Luna (or any worker) may render at two desk positions simultaneously
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1186: activeIds is computed correctly, but syncFromGitHub (App.jsx lines 779-784) still calls setAllWorkers and setRoster as two separate setState calls. Even with React 18 automatic batching in async contexts, the intermediate state window between these two updates can produce a ghost in ghostRoster for one render frame if the data format switches between flat array and {workers, roster}. Observed in desktop screenshot from earlier reviews. Fix: consolidate into a single state object or use useReducer for (allWorkers, roster) pair.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #44] WindowElement bright daytime sky rectangle jarring against dark office
suggestions.description: Office.jsx WindowElement lines 782-786: daytime gradient (bae6fd → f0f9ff, near-white) against the dark office floor reads as a UI glitch. Desktop screenshot confirms a stark white rectangle top-right. Fix: darken the daytime palette (e.g. #7dd3fc → #bae6fd), add a CSS inset box-shadow on .office-window to create a window-frame effect, or add an interior vignette to smooth the contrast with the office background.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] TEAM VIBE stat card orphaned as half-width on mobile
suggestions.description: App.css line 625: .stats-bar at ≤600px uses repeat(2, 1fr). With an odd number of stat cards the last one (TEAM VIBE) renders at 50% width with dead space on the right. Fix: add .stats-bar .stat-card.vibe { grid-column: 1 / -1; } inside that breakpoint.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Paused pill says 'move mouse to refresh' on touch devices
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1148: '⏸ Paused · move mouse to refresh'. App.css line 1019: .polling-paused-pill has pointer-events: none so it cannot be tapped. The touchstart listener (App.jsx line 710) correctly resumes polling on any touch, but the pill copy is wrong and the element is not tappable. Fix: detect touch via window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)') and conditionally show 'Tap anywhere to refresh', or better: remove pointer-events: none and attach the onResume handler to the pill.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] onResume() does not trigger immediate data sync
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1211: onResume only calls setPollingPaused(false) and resets lastActivity. After 10+ minutes idle the user resumes and must wait up to 30 seconds (POLL_INTERVAL_ACTIVE) for fresh data. Fix: add syncFromGitHub() as the third statement in the handler.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Ping reaction copy is third-person — workers narrating themselves
suggestions.description: Office.jsx PING_REACTIONS lines 163-169: Dave's reaction is 'He hears you through the headphones' — Dave is saying this about himself in his own speech bubble. Carlos: 'Check the logs. It is ALWAYS in the logs' reads as external commentary. Fix: rewrite to first-person: 'I hear you through the headphones', 'Check the logs — I already did'.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] tabIndex=0 applied unconditionally to all Character divs
suggestions.description: Office.jsx Character component line 682: tabIndex={0} is hardcoded regardless of whether handleClick is defined. Ghost characters with no interactive handler become keyboard-focusable dead ends for screen reader users. Fix: tabIndex={handleClick ? 0 : -1}.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Activity filter logic duplicated across two render sites
suggestions.description: App.jsx: identical filter-by-type and filter-by-worker chains appear at lines 1283-1293 (section header count IIFE) AND lines 1336-1344 (ActivityLog entries prop). Future filter additions require two edits that will inevitably drift. Fix: extract getFilteredActivity(log, typeFilter, workerFilter) helper, call once, pass result to both.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] IIFE inside JSX for solo spotlight message
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1239: SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES was correctly extracted to module scope (improvement since #44), but the call site still wraps it in an IIFE: `{(() => { const soloKey = ...; return SOLO_SPOTLIGHT_MESSAGES[soloKey]?.[teamVibe.key] ?? ... })()}`. Still allocates a closure on every render. Fix: extract to a named getSoloSpotlightMessage(workerId, vibeKey) helper called outside the JSX return.

suggestions.title: [NEW] Activity log worker filter buttons show '🤖' instead of role emoji
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1331: `{member.emoji ?? '🤖'} {member.name}` — only Luna has an emoji field in DEFAULT_ROSTER; Carlos, Maya, Dave, and Sofia all render as '🤖' 🤖'. Same issue in ActivityLogEntry (line 95): `workerData?.emoji ?? (entry.worker ? '🤖' : null)`. The ROLE_EMOJIS map is already imported. Fix both sites: member.emoji ?? getRoleEmoji(member.role) and workerData?.emoji ?? getRoleEmoji(workerData?.role) ?? '🤖'.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #44] CSS ::after vibe-label for coffee corner clips off mobile viewport right edge
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 1664/1666: `::after` content ' TRIPLE SHOT' and ' STRONG. NOW.' appended to .coffee-corner__label with no overflow control. Desktop and mobile screenshots both confirm the text visibly overflows the right edge of the office floor. .coffee-corner__label (line 375) has no max-width, overflow, or text-overflow. Fix: add overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; max-width: 6rem; white-space: nowrap to .coffee-corner__label.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] cx variable shadows outer scope in ConferenceTable character placement
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 924 and 942: inside meetingWorkers.map() and standupWorkers.map(), `const cx = 52, orbitR = 38` re-declares cx, shadowing the outer `const cx = 52, cy = 52` at line 845. The y-coordinate uses `y = cx - orbitR * Math.cos(rad)` (should be cy). Works today because cx === cy, but is a latent bug if the table ever becomes non-square. Fix: remove inner cx declarations and use the outer cy for y-coordinate calculations.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Speech bubble can clip under tab navigation on mobile
suggestions.description: Office.css: .char has overflow: visible so speech bubbles (position: absolute, bottom: 100%) can extend above the office-floor container and overlap the tab bar on short screens. Structurally inevitable on devices with viewport height under ~700px. Fix: constrain max upward travel of speech bubbles with a max-height or clamp, or add a mobile-specific bottom clamp via env(safe-area-inset-top).

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] plantStageNames array defined but never used
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1071: `const plantStageNames = ['seedling', 'sprout', 'small-plant', 'leafy', 'blooming']` is declared inside the Office component and never referenced — plantStage uses hardcoded string literals directly. Remove the dead array.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] ProgressBar receives updatedAt prop it never uses
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 193: updatedAt is destructured in the function signature but never read — the ETA calculation uses only startedAt. Line 346 still passes updatedAt={worker.updated_at}. Remove updatedAt from the signature and the call site.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Three static constants declared inside Character component body
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 510, 526-533: IDLE_BUBBLE_EMOJIS, ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES, and WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES are re-declared on every Character render. With 5+ workers rendering simultaneously this is unnecessary repeated allocation. Move all three to module scope alongside PING_REACTIONS and WORKER_AWAY_DESK_LABELS.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] VibeSparkline SVG has duplicate accessible text via aria-label and title element
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 401-402: the SVG has both aria-label and a <title> child element with identical generated text. Screen readers may announce the label twice. Fix: remove the aria-label from the SVG element and let the title child serve as the accessible name per SVG accessibility spec (aria-labelledby pointing to the title id is cleaner).

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] conf-fire-glow SVG filter uses hardcoded DOM ID
suggestions.description: Office.jsx ConferenceTable line 851: filter id='conf-fire-glow' is hardcoded. In environments with multiple Office instances (testing, SSR, React strict mode double-invoke), duplicate IDs cause silent filter-reference failures in some browsers. Fix: use React's useId() hook (already imported in Office.jsx line 1) to generate a unique ID per instance.

suggestions.title: [CARRY-OVER #42] Activity log entry React key appends array index as tiebreaker
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 151: key={entry.timestamp + entry.worker + i} — appending i makes keys shift when entries are filtered or new entries prepend the array, causing unnecessary unmount/remount cycles. Fix: use a stable composite key such as `${entry.timestamp}:${entry.worker ?? 'system'}:${entry.type}`.

summary: Review #45 of Bloberto's Office. One fix landed: idle character wander boundary is now properly constrained to x: 15–71%, y: 73–87%, and the screenshot confirms characters no longer escape the office floor. Progress, such as it is. The four HIGH bugs — undefined emoji, NaN progress bar, bad aria-label, stale useMemo Date.now() — remain untouched for the third or fourth review running. The WindowElement daytime rectangle is now confirmed in two consecutive screenshot sessions and still glares like a production incident nobody filed. The coffee corner 'TRIPLE SHOT' overflow is visible in both desktop and mobile screenshots and still has no max-width. A new medium issue was identified: the activity log worker filter buttons and log entries both resolve worker emoji to '🤖' instead of using the ROLE_EMOJIS map that's already imported. The IIFE in the solo spotlight render is partially addressed (data was extracted to module scope) but the closure wrapper itself was not removed. Twenty-one of twenty-two carry-overs remain open. The codebase is genuinely fun and polished to look at; it just has a persistent backlog of small fixes that keep not making it into PRs.
carry_overs_from_44: 21 of 22 suggestions from #44 carry over. The one fix: idle wander boundary (carry-over #43) — wander keyframes now explicitly bounded within the office floor with a code comment confirming the intent. NOT fixed: stuck emoji undefined (HIGH x3), NaN progress bar (HIGH x3), aria-label undefined% (HIGH x2), stale Date.now() useMemo (HIGH x3), Luna double render (MEDIUM x1), daytime window contrast (MEDIUM x1), TEAM VIBE half-width mobile (MEDIUM x3), paused pill touch copy (MEDIUM x3), onResume no sync (MEDIUM x3), ping reaction third-person (MEDIUM x3), unconditional tabIndex (MEDIUM x3), duplicated filter logic (MEDIUM x3), IIFE solo spotlight (MEDIUM x3 — partial data extraction but closure wrapper remains), coffee corner label clip (LOW x1), cx variable shadow (LOW x2), speech bubble tab overlap (LOW x3), plantStageNames dead code (LOW x3), updatedAt unused prop (LOW x3), constants inside Character (LOW x3), VibeSparkline duplicate text (LOW x3), hardcoded filter ID (LOW x3), unstable activity log key (LOW x3).
